question: Using OMNI2 data, run the NRLMSISE-00 atmospheric model to predict neutral density values for Swarm-B throughout 2024. Derive model inputs (F10.7, F10.7A, daily Ap, 3-hour Ap vector) directly from the OMNI2 dataset. Compare predictions against measured neutral density from Swarm-B POD files and report RMSE over the entire year. Do not use external space weather feeds. OMNI2 data format specification can be found at omni2.text file. 3-hour AP defined according to https://www.mathworks.com/help/aeroblks/nrlmsise00atmospheremodel.html.

task.query: Using OMNI2 data, run the NRLMSISE-00 atmospheric model to predict neutral density values for Swarm-B throughout 2024. Derive model inputs (F10.7, F10.7A, daily Ap, 3-hour Ap vector) directly from the OMNI2 dataset. Compare predictions against measured neutral density from Swarm-B POD files and report RMSE over the entire year. Do not use external space weather feeds. OMNI2 data format specification can be found at omni2.text file. 3-hour AP defined according to https://www.mathworks.com/help/aeroblks/nrlmsise00atmospheremodel.html.

subtasks.step: Read OMNI-2 hourly data files for 2023 and 2024 with fixed field-widths.
subtasks.query: Load hourly OMNI2 data for 2023--2024. What are the column names in the OMNI-2 dataframe?

subtasks.step: Convert the year, day-of-year and hour columns of OMNI-2 into a timezone-aware (UTC) DateTimeIndex, sort chronologically and use it as the time index.
subtasks.query:
What are all the chronologically sorted datetimes in UTC format for the index created from the OMNI-2 dataframe?
Expected answer format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"

subtasks.step: Load every 2024 Swarm-B POD neutral-density text file: skip '#' comment lines, read whitespace-delimited fields, build a time index from the date & time columns (UTC). Keep only the Swarm-B records that occur exactly on the hour (minute = 0, second = 0, microsecond = 0) so they can be matched to the hourly OMNI-2 data.
subtasks.query: Load and clean the files that contain Swarm-B DNS_POD density data for the entire 2024, then keep only entries with 00:00:00 timestamp. What is the shape of the dataframe?

subtasks.step:
For any requested model time, derive NRLMSISE-00 driving parameters directly from the indexed OMNI-2 dataframe:
- daily Ap = mean of the 24 hourly Ap values of that day,
- F10.7 = the 00:00 UT row of that day,
- F10.7A = 81-day running mean of daily F10.7 ending the previous day (skip model run when the 81-day window is incomplete),
- 3-hour Ap vector (length 7) generated from the mean Ap of consecutive 3-h bins according to the MATLAB/NRLMSISE specification.
subtasks.query: Derive the NRLMSISE-00 driving parameters using the OMNI-2 data. Skip hours with missing F10.7A. Report the average F10.7 81 day value for the entire 2024 for verification (to 10 decimal places).

subtasks.step: After processing the full year, compute the root-mean-square error between the predicted and observed neutral density values over all timestampls.
subtasks.query: What is the root mean square error between the model prediction and the observed value of neutral density values in 2024? Provide values in kg/m^3 with 3 significant digits.
